channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wealthy beach town run on migrant labor says enclave handcuffed by blue state's sanctuary law
item.description: East Hampton, New York, town leaders say their police department will enforce criminal law but lacks authority to detain illegals over civil immigration matters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge blocks Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing sensitive US Treasury Department material
item.description: A federal judge early Saturday blocked Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans. U.S. District Judge Paul A.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A 15-year-old went to a Brooklyn parade. The NYPD wrongly accused him of a mass shooting
item.description: A New York teen is trying to put his life back together after he was wrongly identified as a suspect in a mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives in programs receiving federal money has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, TERRY CHEA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Expert explains how a firing squad works
item.description: "It's very rapid. You're completely unconscious very quickly," said firing squad expert Dr. James Williams.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Generations later, a remedy to destroying Black neighborhoods is fulfilled in Michigan
item.description: HAMTRAMCK, Mich. (AP) — Leslie Knox was a young girl in the 1960s when her Detroit-area city was accused of destroying neighborhoods to get rid of Black residents.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A hidden gem of whitewater rafting faces an uncertain future after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ERWIN, Tenn. (AP) — After 24 years of guiding whitewater trips on the Nolichucky River Gorge for other companies, Patrick Mannion finally received a permit last year to operate his own outfitter business.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia police officer shot, killed while responding to shopping center
item.description: A Georgia police officer was shot and killed Friday night when responding to a report of a suspicious person at a shopping center in the City of Roswell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board were dead, authorities said. The crash was one of the deadliest in the state in the last 25 years.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: West Virginia leads 22 states suing over New York Climate Change Superfund Act
item.description: West Virginia led the charge against New York’s Climate Change Superfund Act, suing to block the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, Fox 5

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: For high school sports, decisions loom: Follow Trump or state law on transgender athletes
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota's governing body for high school sports says it will follow state law — not President Donald Trump's executive order — and continue to allow transgender athletes to compete in prep athletics.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KRON4

item.title: Claims about USAID funding are spreading online. Many are not based on facts
item.description: As the Trump administration moves to dismantle the U.S. Agency for International Development, false and misleading information is being pushed on social media to support the change, much of it spread by the administration and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alaska Legislature asks Trump to retain Denali's name instead of change it to Mount McKinley
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The Alaska Legislature passed a resolution Friday urging President Donald Trump to reverse course and retain the name of North America’s tallest peak as Denali rather than change it to Mount McKinley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: NFL has no plans to end DEI practices, commissioner says
item.description: The NFL is committed to sticking to its DEI policies despite a change in its Super Bowl on-field messaging campaign
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Starbucks offering free coffee Monday after the Super Bowl: How to get yours
item.description: Starbucks is offering a free pick-me-up on Monday for football fans who might be staying up late celebrating (or fuming over) the results of the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jane Alvarez-Wertz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration orders federal agencies to provide lists of underperforming employees
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has ordered all federal departments and agencies to provide lists of employees who are underperforming, as it seeks to shrink the workforce and awaits a court ruling related to its deferred resignation offers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fetterman says he'll vote against RFK Jr., Gabbard nominations
item.description: "I have voted against moving forward to the confirmation of Ms. Gabbard and Mr. Kennedy, and will be voting NO on their confirmations.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: Baseball star Shohei Ohtani’s ex-interpreter is sentenced to nearly 5 years in sports betting case
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — The former interpreter for baseball star Shohei Ohtani was sentenced Thursday to nearly five years in prison for bank and tax fraud after he stole nearly $17 million from the Los Angeles Dodgers player’s bank account.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump blames 'obsolete' US air traffic control system for the plane and chopper collision near DC
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday blamed the deadly collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter last week on what he called an “obsolete” computer system used by U.S. air traffic controllers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate installs Russell Vought as Trump’s next budget chief
item.description: The Senate voted 53-47 along party lines to confirm Project 2025 architect Russell Vought on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Reporter meets Eaton Fire victims whose home he helped save on live TV
item.description: “The ember cast from this blaze is just astonishing, you can see just how ferocious the wind is,” John Fenoglio said in his live report. “It looks like the back of this house is catching – oh my God.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: 5 questions about DOGE's access to federal payment systems
item.description: Questions are swirling about how much access Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has to Americans’ personal data and sensitive federal payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Arrest made in home invasion where men dressed as Amazon workers
item.description: The Savannah Police Department (SPD) has made an arrest in the home invasion that sent one man to the hospital in early January.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Austin ISD policy prevents parents from seeing video of alleged crimes against kids
item.description: He said his first-grade teacher trapped him behind a classroom door at Margaret T. Reilly Elementary School and then pushed on it with his weight.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Wiley, NewsNation

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: (KTLA) - Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year. This year, the chain is offering 3,000 passes that will be exclusively available to Club Applebee’s members.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: New NASA imagery shows large asteroid that could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: A dozen West Point cadet clubs ordered to disband, all others paused
item.description: The U.S. Military Academy at West Point, New York, has ordered a dozen cadet extracurricular and social clubs to immediately and permanently "cease all activities," with all affected groups centered on gender, race and ethnicity, the school acknowledged Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Police seek motive after a suspect was arrested for a fatal shooting at an Ohio cosmetics warehouse
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A man accused of shooting to death a coworker and wounding five others at an Ohio warehouse has spoken with police as they probe for a motive, authorities said Wednesday, hours after he was arrested in an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Oregon Zoo welcomes a new baby elephant
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — The Oregon Zoo in Portland has welcomed its newest addition, a baby elephant. Thirty-year-old Asian elephant Rose-Tu gave birth on Saturday after 20 months of pregnancy, the zoo said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
